description: Feature Slices Architecture & Large File Refactor — restructure apps to use feature slices, split oversized files, and split Convex backend

userStories.title: Split convex/elevators.ts into domain modules
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the 36 KB backend file split into domain-focused modules in convex/elevators/ so queries are easy to find.
acceptanceCriteria: convex/elevators.ts removed and replaced with convex/elevators/ directory
convex/elevators/crud.ts contains: get, search, create, update, archive, checkElevatorNumber
convex/elevators/listing.ts contains: list, exportData
convex/elevators/analytics.ts contains: stats, chartData
convex/elevators/modernization.ts contains: timeline, budget, priorityList, measures (no 'modernization' prefix on function names)
convex/elevators/maintenance.ts contains: inspectionCalendar, companies, emergencyPhoneStatus, inspectionList, todaysElevators
convex/elevators/helpers.ts contains: autoAddSuggestedValues, fetchAndFilter, filterArgs schema, org name cache (internal helpers, not exported via api)
All frontend imports in both admin and client apps updated to new API paths (e.g. api.elevators.get -> api.elevators.crud.get, api.elevators.list -> api.elevators.listing.list, etc.)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Do this FIRST while frontend files are still monoliths — fewer files to update API paths in. Grep for all 'api.elevators.' references across both apps. Convex file-based routing maps convex/elevators/crud.ts -> api.elevators.crud.get. The helpers.ts file should use internalQuery/internalMutation or just be a regular TS file with exported helpers — check how the existing code uses these functions.

userStories.title: Admin app feature/shared folder structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the admin app restructured with features/ and shared/ directories so code is organized by domain.
acceptanceCriteria: src/features/ directory created with slices: elevator/, maintenance/, modernization/, register/, import/, cms/
Each feature slice has components/ subdirectory (others created as needed, not empty)
src/shared/components/, src/shared/hooks/, src/shared/lib/ directories created
Existing src/components/ files moved to src/shared/components/ (except CMS form components)
CMS form components (startsida-form.tsx, om-oss-form.tsx, tjanster-form.tsx, kontakt-form.tsx) moved to src/features/cms/components/
Existing src/lib/ files moved to src/shared/lib/
All imports updated to new paths — no broken references
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Only move existing shared files (components/, lib/) into shared/. Do NOT extract feature-specific code from route files yet — that happens in later stories (US-005 through US-010). CMS forms are already domain-specific so they go directly into features/cms/.

userStories.title: Client app feature/shared folder structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the client app restructured with features/ and shared/ directories so code is organized by domain.
acceptanceCriteria: src/features/ directory created with slices: elevator/, maintenance/, modernization/, register/
Each feature slice has components/ subdirectory (others created as needed, not empty)
src/shared/components/, src/shared/hooks/, src/shared/lib/ directories created
Existing src/components/ files moved to src/shared/components/
Existing src/lib/ files moved to src/shared/lib/
All imports updated to new paths — no broken references
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Same pattern as admin app (US-002). Only move existing shared files, do not extract feature-specific code from route files yet.

userStories.title: Move reusable UI components to packages/ui
userStories.description: As a developer, I want generic UI components in the shared package so both apps can import them.
acceptanceCriteria: MultiSelectFilter component added to packages/ui/src/components/ui/ with generic props (no domain-specific logic)
SortHeader component added to packages/ui/src/components/ui/ with generic props
StatCard component added to packages/ui/src/components/ui/ with generic props
UploadZone component added to packages/ui/src/components/ui/ with generic props
All 4 components exported from package index
Imports in admin and client apps updated to use @elevatorbud/ui imports
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Find these components in admin/client route files. Generalize their props (remove domain-specific types, use generic names). Add to packages/ui following existing shadcn component patterns. Update all import sites in both apps.

userStories.title: Split elevator edit form into section components
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the 52 KB edit form split into focused section components so each is easy to understand and modify.
acceptanceCriteria: hiss.$id.redigera.tsx route file is a thin orchestrator that imports and composes section components

Extracted to features/elevator/components/: BasicInfoSection, TechnicalSpecsSection, DoorsAndCabSection, MachinerySection, InspectionSection, ModernizationSection, EmergencyPhoneSection, CommentsSection
useSuggestions hook extracted to features/elevator/hooks/use-suggestions.ts
Draft persistence logic extracted to features/elevator/hooks/use-draft-persistence.ts
Form converters (serverToFormValues, toOptionalString, toOptionalNumber) extracted to features/elevator/utils/form-converters.ts
Form values type extracted to features/elevator/types.ts
Route file under 10 KB
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Section components receive the TanStack Form instance via props and render fields. These same section components will be reused by the wizard in US-006. Keep localStorage draft keys stable (draft-hiss-{id}). All paths are relative to apps/admin/src/.

userStories.title: Split new elevator wizard into step components
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the 48 KB wizard split into step components and a reusable wizard shell.
acceptanceCriteria: ny.tsx route file is a thin orchestrator importing wizard and step components
Wizard container extracted to features/elevator/components/ElevatorWizard.tsx (step navigation, progress indicator)
Each of the 9 steps extracted as separate components in features/elevator/components/wizard-steps/
Step components reuse the same section components from US-005 where fields overlap
Review step (Step 9) is its own component showing a summary
Shared hooks (useSuggestions, useDraftPersistence) reused from US-005 extractions
Route file under 5 KB
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005 — reuse the same section components extracted there. The wizard renders one section per step; the edit page renders all sections. Keep localStorage draft key stable (draft-ny-hiss).

userStories.title: Split admin import page into stage components
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the 32 KB import wizard split into stage components.
acceptanceCriteria: admin.import.tsx route file is a thin orchestrator
Extracted to features/import/components/: UploadStage, ColumnMappingStage, PreviewStage, ImportingStage, ResultStage
Import state machine logic extracted to features/import/hooks/use-import-machine.ts
StatCard and UploadZone imported from @elevatorbud/ui (moved in US-004)
Route file under 5 KB
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: StatCard and UploadZone should already be in packages/ui from US-004 — import from @elevatorbud/ui. Focus on extracting the 5 import stages and the state machine hook.

userStories.title: Split admin register page into feature components
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the admin register page split so the route file is a thin orchestrator.
acceptanceCriteria: admin register.tsx route file composes feature components from features/register/
Table, filter, and search logic extracted to features/register/components/
MultiSelectFilter and SortHeader imported from @elevatorbud/ui (moved in US-004)
Route file under 10 KB
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Admin register has edit links and org context that client version does not — keep admin-specific logic in admin feature components. Do NOT share components with client app.

userStories.title: Split admin maintenance page into feature components
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the admin maintenance page split so the route file is a thin orchestrator.
acceptanceCriteria: admin underhall.tsx route file composes feature components from features/maintenance/
Chart components and data cards extracted to features/maintenance/components/
Route file under 10 KB
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Admin maintenance page may have org selector context. Keep admin-specific logic in admin feature components, do NOT share with client app.
